summary: newBatchID suffixes 4 random hex bytes so concurrent send-batch invocations within the same second produce distinct batch IDs.
reason: Generator-emitted newBatchID used seconds-resolution timestamps only. Two send-batch invocations starting within the same second (CI jobs, scripts, fast retries) would produce identical IDs; persistBatch then silently overwrites the earlier batch, leaving the first unrecoverable by sms reconcile. Surfaced by Greptile P2 in the PR #417 review.
validated_outcome: go build ./... succeeds; go test ./... passes.
